```html
Consultation Note

I expect that the following will be carried over directly from the intake form or EMR:

  • Patient Name: Hassan
  • Patient Date of Birth: Invalid Date
  • Location: Hand Surgery Clinic, Main Campus
  • Date of Service: 11/23/2025

Corresponding Form

  • MRN: 1234567
  • Assessment: Hassan presents with ongoing pain and limited function in the right hand following a traumatic injury sustained approximately two months ago. The patient has undergone initial conservative management with partial improvement.
  • Plan:
  • Medications: Ordered Antibiotics
  • Therapy: Ordered
  • Outside Imaging or Nerve Study: Prescription Provided for MRI of the right hand
  • Splint: Options should be provided
    • Type: Custom thermoplastic splint for the right hand
  • Injections: Fluoroscopy guided
    • Location: Right wrist
    • Medication: Kenalog
  • Work/School Status: 10lbs restriction
  • Specific Comments: Patient is advised to avoid activities that exacerbate symptoms and to follow up in two weeks for reassessment.

Chief Complaint:

Hassan is experiencing persistent pain and reduced function in the right hand following a traumatic injury.

HPI:

Hassan, a 22-year-old male, sustained a traumatic injury to his right hand approximately two months ago. Initial management included immobilization and analgesics, which provided partial relief. The patient reports ongoing pain and difficulty with hand movements, particularly affecting his ability to perform daily tasks. There is no known history of allergies. The injury is localized to the right hand, with no other systemic symptoms reported. Previous imaging studies indicated soft tissue swelling, but no fractures were identified.

Objective:

Heart: Regular rate and rhythm as assessed by palpating the radial artery. Lungs: Regular respiratory rate and pattern, no respiratory distress observed. Right hand: Swelling and tenderness present, reduced range of motion noted. No signs of infection or open wounds. Neurological examination of the right hand reveals decreased grip strength and mild sensory deficits in the distribution of the median nerve.

Assessment:

Hassan is experiencing post-traumatic pain and functional limitation in the right hand, likely due to soft tissue injury and possible nerve involvement.

  • 1. Post-traumatic pain in the right hand - ICD-10: M79.641
  • 2. Soft tissue injury of the right hand - ICD-10: S69.91XA
  • 3. Possible median nerve involvement - ICD-10: G56.00

Plan:

  1. Medications
    • Prescribe a course of antibiotics to prevent any potential infection.
  2. Therapy
    • Initiate occupational therapy focusing on hand function improvement.
  3. Imaging
    • Order MRI of the right hand to assess soft tissue and nerve status.
  4. Splint
    • Provide a custom thermoplastic splint to support the right hand.
  5. Injections
    • Administer fluoroscopy-guided Kenalog injection to the right wrist for inflammation control.
  6. Work/School Status
    • Impose a 10lbs lifting restriction to prevent exacerbation of symptoms.
  7. Follow-up
    • Schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to evaluate progress and adjust the treatment plan as necessary.

```
